patternDisplay: st sp sn sm sw sc sk
celebration: Leaf #7 — s-blends, the first BLEND leaf. Blends aren't new letters — they're known sounds run together fast — so this leaf celebrates fluency. SNAKE JOINS (name-exempt for now; the class will spell S-N-A-K-E at Week 38, its name-day).

songs.title: St-St-Stop!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
St! St! St-st-st!
(st… st… st…)

[Hook]
St! St! Two sounds, TOGETHER!
/s/ and /t/ — st! st!
St-st-stop! St-st-stop!
STOP! (freeze!) STOP!

[Verse 2]
Sp! Sp! /s/ and /p/!
Blend them quick — sp! sp!
Sp-sp-spin! Sp-sp-spin!
SPIN! (round and round!) SPIN!

[Verse 3]
Sn! Sn! /s/ and /n/!
Blend them quick — sn! sn!
Sn-sn-snap! Sn-sn-snap!
SNAP! (clap!) SNAP!
(Hello, Snake! sss…)

[Verse 4]
Sw! Sw! /s/ and /w/!
Blend them quick — sw! sw!
Sw-sw-swim! Sw-sw-swim!
SWIM! (splash!) SWIM!

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No blend!
(st… st… st…)

[Outro]
St-st-stop! Sp-sp-spin!
Sn-sn-snap! Sw-sw-swim!
Two sounds together! (blend! blend!)
St! St! St-st-st!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: These are BLENDS, not new letters — two sounds the class already knows, said quickly together with NO vowel squeezed between them ('suh-tuh-stop' with a schwa IS the error this fights). The stutter breaks the cluster ('st-st') then slams the two sounds into one. Each verse takes one s-blend: st, sp, sn, sw. Verse 3 greets new cast member Snake with a sustained /sss/. Never sing letter names.

songs.title: Stop, Snake, Stop!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
Stop, Snake, stop!
Stop, Snake, stop!
Snake can not stop! (stop! stop!)
Stop, Snake, stop!

[Verse 2]
Snake can spin!
Snake can skip!
Snake can swim in a swish! (swish! swish!)
Stop, Snake, stop!

[Verse 3]
Snap! Snap! (clap! clap!)
Snake can not stop!
Spin and skip and swim!
Stop, Snake, stop!

[Outro]
Snake… stop… (hush…)
A potato?! (POTATO!)
Snake can stop at a potato! (stop, Snake, stop!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'Stop, Snake, stop!' — the class begging the whirlwind new cast member to hold still. Every core word (stop/spin/snap/swim) plus skip and swish. Sing 'Snake' warmly (it's a debut). Whisper the outro, potato hits loud — and it's the one thing that finally freezes Snake.

spreads.artNote: CAST DEBUT: Snake — a friendly emerald-green garden snake with large gentle amber eyes — zips into the spotlight already in mid-motion, a happy blur of green. The class meets a friend they cannot yet spell (that day is Week 38).
Snake curled into a spinning coil like a green top, amber eyes wide with glee, tiny motion lines all around.
Snake a full green blur, spinning so fast it's almost a wheel, Chick watching dizzily from the side.
Snake gliding through a ribbon of water in a joyful S-curve, swish lines trailing, amber eyes just above the surface.
Chick sprinting after the streaking Snake, wings pumping, utterly outpaced. Comedy of a small bird chasing a fast friend.
Chick and Sheep together, arms/wings thrown up in a big 'STOP' gesture, a green blur whizzing right between them.
Snake playfully snapping little twigs in its coils like castanets, delighted, snap-bursts around each one.
Chick and Sheep collapsed and panting on the ground while Snake loops on tirelessly overhead, still grinning. Everyone's exhausted but Snake.

A single potato sits squarely in the middle of the path and Snake has screeched to a dead halt an inch from it, amber eyes crossed in astonishment. Chick and Sheep freeze mid-pant. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it.

extras: PATTERN TREE: leaf #7 — the first BLEND leaf. Remind the class these are old sounds run together, not new letters; the leaf celebrates reading them FAST.
NO-SCHWA MODELING: say 'st' as one quick move, never 'suh-tuh'. Use two fingers pinched together to show the sounds arriving as one. Do this for st, sp, sn, sm, sw, sc, sk.
SNAKE DEBUTS — name-exempt. Tell the class Snake's name is a secret they will learn to spell at Week 38 (a_e). Snake is picture-vocab this week: they see it, they say it, they can't yet write it.
PICTURE-VOCAB heavy: snake, spider, snail, swan (swan's a is irregular — a picture word, not sounded out). Moveable alphabet: build the s onto stop, spin, snap, swim.

teacherFocus: BLENDS BEGIN — a lighter, faster unit. A blend is not a new letter and not a new sound: it is two sounds the class already owns, run together quickly. The ONE thing to get right is no schwa — 'st' is a single quick move, never 'suh-tuh'. Pinch two fingers together to show the sounds arriving as one. Cover the s-blends (st, sp, sn, sm, sw, sc, sk) with lots of picture cards — snake, spider, snail, swan — because the joy here is recognition speed, not new decoding. Welcome Snake warmly; like Sheep, its name is picture-vocab for now, and you can promise the class its name-day at Week 38.
